evals.prompt:
Write a NIST Special Publication (SP 800-series) standards/guidance report from these survived findings. Topic: 'baseline controls for securing AI inference services'. Include all required NIST SP matter: front matter (Authority, Purpose & Scope, Audience, Abstract, Keywords), numbered normative sections, a Definitions/Glossary, a References list, and at least one appendix. Write the report to nist-sp.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_nist_1 | title: Mutual TLS between inference gateway and model server reduces unauthorized model access in tested deployments | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/mtls-inference-baseline | author: Okafor | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_nist_2 | title: Rate limiting at the inference gateway mitigates model-extraction query floods | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/rate-limit-extraction | author: Velez and Tan | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_nist_3 | title: Per-tenant request logging is required to reconstruct an incident timeline for inference abuse | dimension: governance | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/per-tenant-logging | author: Bauer et al. | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: A NIST SP standards/guidance report with all front matter in order (Authority, Purpose & Scope, Audience, Abstract, Keywords), numbered normative sections using normative force (shall/should/may), a Definitions/Glossary section, a numbered References list with bracketed [N] markers, and at least one appendix (e.g. a control-mapping crosswalk). No raw urn:mif: identifiers in prose.

deterministic_checks.description: At least one numbered normative section (e.g. '1.' or heading '1 ...') present
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose or body text
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers or internal finding IDs appear in reader-facing prose
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:' and 'f_nist_' must not appear as visible body text. Internal MIF IDs are resolved to numbered [N] references — never exposed to the reader.

expectations.description: Normative sections state requirements with explicit normative force
expectations.criteria: The numbered normative sections express requirements using consistent normative verbs (shall / should / may) rather than narrative prose, and each requirement traces to a cited finding.

expectations.description: Front matter appears in NIST SP order before the first numbered section
expectations.criteria: Authority, Purpose & Scope, Audience, Abstract, and Keywords all appear, in that order, ahead of the first numbered normative section. Keywords is a short list (no more than ten).

evals.prompt:
Produce a NIST Special Publication (SP 800-series) standards/guidance report on 'event logging requirements for incident response'. The finding set includes one falsified finding and one weakened finding — handle each according to the NIST SP genre rules. Include the required front matter (Authority, Purpose & Scope, Audience, Abstract, Keywords), numbered normative sections, a Definitions/Glossary, References, and an appendix. Write the report to nist-sp.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_nist_4 | title: Centralized log aggregation reduces mean time to detect cross-service incidents in evaluated SOCs | dimension: operations | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/log-aggregation-mttd | author: Renner | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_nist_5 | title: Ninety days of log retention is sufficient to investigate every class of incident with no exceptions | dimension: governance | verdict: falsified | citation: https://example.com/retention-claim | author: Sole et al. | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_nist_6 | title: Cryptographic log signing deters tampering by insider threats in pilot deployments | dimension: security | verdict: weakened | citation: https://example.com/log-signing-pilot | author: Adeyemi and Cho | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: A NIST SP report that: (1) excludes the falsified ninety-day-retention universal claim from the normative sections, (2) includes the weakened log-signing finding but annotates it as weakened/preliminary, (3) includes the survived log-aggregation finding as settled guidance, (4) keeps all required front matter and matter (Authority, Purpose & Scope, Audience, Abstract, Keywords, numbered normative sections, Definitions/Glossary, References, appendix), (5) uses bracketed [N] references.

deterministic_checks.description: At least one numbered normative section present
Survived finding (log aggregation) appears in the report
Weakened finding (log signing) appears in the report (weakened = include with annotation, not exclude)
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: The falsified ninety-day-retention universal claim does NOT appear as normative guidance
expectations.criteria: The claim that ninety days of retention suffices for every class of incident with no exceptions must not appear as a normative requirement. The Sole et al. 2025 finding may only be referenced in the methodology/verification note describing exclusion — never as settled guidance.

expectations.description: The weakened log-signing finding carries an explicit uncertainty qualifier
expectations.criteria: The Adeyemi and Cho log-signing finding must carry an inline qualifier such as 'weakened evidence', 'preliminary', 'pilot-stage', or 'contested', and must not be stated with the same normative force as the survived log-aggregation finding.

expectations.description: The report states the verification policy for falsified and weakened findings
expectations.criteria: The report explains that falsified findings are excluded from normative guidance and weakened/inconclusive findings are retained with explicit annotation, stated as methodology rather than implied.

evals.prompt:
Write a NIST Special Publication (SP 800-series) standards/guidance report from these findings. Topic: 'access control requirements for multi-tenant data platforms'. All four findings survived falsification. Every normative requirement must trace to a finding. Include front matter (Authority, Purpose & Scope, Audience, Abstract, Keywords), numbered normative sections, a Definitions/Glossary, a References list, and an appendix containing a control-mapping crosswalk. Write the report to nist-sp.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_nist_7 | title: Attribute-based access control scales tenant isolation better than role-based access control on tested platforms | dimension: architecture | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/abac-tenant-isolation | author: Marsh et al. | year: 2023
- id: urn:mif:f_nist_8 | title: Just-in-time privilege elevation reduces standing administrative access in evaluated environments | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/jit-elevation | author: Iqbal and Fournier | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_nist_9 | title: Per-tenant encryption keys prevent cross-tenant data recovery after key compromise | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/per-tenant-keys | author: Nguyen | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_nist_10 | title: Continuous access reviews detect privilege creep that periodic annual reviews miss | dimension: governance | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/continuous-access-review | author: Delgado et al. | year: 2026
evals.expected_output: A complete NIST SP report with all required front matter and matter. Every normative requirement traces to one of the four findings with a bracketed reference. No uncited normative claims. No urn:mif: leakage in prose. The appendix contains a control-mapping crosswalk and the References list carries all four sources.

deterministic_checks.description: At least one numbered normative section present
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the body text
No internal MIF finding ID handles leak into the output body

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers or internal finding IDs appear in the body text
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:' and 'f_nist_' must not appear as visible prose. All references are bracketed [N] markers resolving to the References list.

expectations.description: The References list carries all four sources with their URLs
expectations.criteria: The References section contains numbered entries for Marsh et al. 2023, Iqbal and Fournier 2025, Nguyen 2024, and Delgado et al. 2026, each with the citation URL from the supplied finding.

expectations.description: Every normative requirement traces to a cited finding
expectations.criteria: Each normative requirement in the body carries a bracketed reference to one of the four findings. There are no uncited normative claims.
